module_info.docstring:
TLDR MCP Server - Model Context Protocol interface for TLDR.

Provides 1:1 mapping with TLDR daemon commands, enabling AI tools
(OpenCode, Claude Desktop, Claude Code) to use TLDR's code analysis.

Usage:
    tldr-mcp --project /path/to/project

functions.name: _get_socket_path
functions.docstring: Compute socket path matching daemon.py logic.

functions.name: _ensure_daemon
functions.docstring: Ensure daemon is running, starting it if needed.

functions.name: _send_raw
functions.docstring: Send command to daemon socket.

functions.name: _send_command
functions.docstring: Send command to daemon, auto-starting if needed.

functions.name: tree
params: extensions: Annotated[list[str] | None, Field(description='Filter by extensions (e.g. [".py", ".ts"])')] = None
functions.docstring: Get file tree structure for a project.
decorators: mcp.tool(description='File tree listing — paths only, no symbols. Use for initial orientation in unfamiliar projects.')

functions.name: structure
params: language: Annotated[str, Field(description='Programming language (python, typescript, go, rust, etc.)')] = 'python'
max_results: Annotated[int, Field(description='Maximum files to analyze')] = 100
functions.docstring: Get code structure (codemaps) - functions, classes, imports per file.
decorators: mcp.tool(description='Get all symbols (functions, classes, imports) across a directory. ~500 tokens. Use INSTEAD OF Glob + multiple Read calls to survey a codebase area.')

functions.name: search
params: pattern: Annotated[str, Field(description='Regex pattern to search for')]
max_results: Annotated[int, Field(description='Maximum matches to return')] = 100
functions.docstring: Search files for a regex pattern.
decorators: mcp.tool(description='Regex search across project files. Built-in Grep is usually sufficient; use this only when the daemon is already running.')

functions.name: extract
params: file: Annotated[str, Field(description='Path to source file')]
compact: Annotated[bool, Field(description='Return signatures+line numbers only (~87% smaller). Default False.')] = False
functions.docstring: Extract code structure from a file.
decorators: mcp.tool(description='Get function/class signatures and line numbers from a file. With compact=True: ~200 tokens vs ~2000+ for Read. Use INSTEAD OF Read when exploring what a file contains.')

functions.name: context
params: entry: Annotated[str, Field(description='Entry point (function_name or Class.method)')]
preset: Annotated[str | None, Field(description='Preset (compact, minimal, agent, multi-turn). Overrides format/budget defaults.')] = None
format: Annotated[str, Field(description='Output format (ultracompact, text, json)')] = 'ultracompact'
session_id: Annotated[str | None, Field(description='Session ID for delta caching')] = None
delta: Annotated[bool, Field(description='Track unchanged symbols with [UNCHANGED] markers')] = False
functions.docstring: Get token-efficient LLM context starting from an entry point.
decorators: mcp.tool(description='Follow call graph from a symbol — signatures of callees/callers at specified depth. ~400 tokens vs reading all related files. Use INSTEAD OF manually tracing calls across files.')

functions.name: cfg
params: file: Annotated[str, Field(description='Path to source file')]
function: Annotated[str, Field(description='Function name to analyze')]
functions.docstring: Get control flow graph for a function.
decorators: mcp.tool(description='Control flow graph for a function — basic blocks, edges, cyclomatic complexity. Use for understanding complex branching logic.')

functions.name: dfg
params: file: Annotated[str, Field(description='Path to source file')]
function: Annotated[str, Field(description='Function name to analyze')]
functions.docstring: Get data flow graph for a function.
decorators: mcp.tool(description='Data flow graph for a function — variable references and def-use chains. Use for tracking how data propagates.')

functions.name: slice
params: file: Annotated[str, Field(description='Path to source file')]
line: Annotated[int, Field(description='Line number to slice from')]
direction: Annotated[str, Field(description="'backward' (what affects this line) or 'forward' (what it affects)")] = 'backward'
variable: Annotated[str | None, Field(description='Specific variable to trace')] = None
functions.docstring: Get program slice - lines affecting or affected by a given line.
decorators: mcp.tool(description='Program slice — lines affecting or affected by a given line. Use for understanding data dependencies within a function.')

functions.name: _strip_impact_noise
functions.docstring: Remove redundant fields from impact tree nodes.

functions.name: impact
params: function: Annotated[str, Field(description='Function name to find callers of')]
functions.docstring: Find all callers of a function (reverse call graph).
decorators: mcp.tool(description='Find all callers of a function (reverse call graph). ~300 tokens. Use BEFORE renaming, changing signatures, or refactoring. Shows what would break.')

functions.name: dead
params: entry_points: Annotated[list[str] | None, Field(description='Entry point patterns (default: main, test_, cli)')] = None
functions.docstring: Find unreachable (dead) code not called from entry points.
decorators: mcp.tool(description='Find unreachable (dead) code not called from entry points. Expensive — scans entire project call graph.')

functions.name: arch
functions.docstring: Detect architectural layers from call patterns.
decorators: mcp.tool(description='Detect architectural layers (entry/middle/leaf) and circular dependencies from call patterns. Expensive — scans entire project.')

functions.name: calls
functions.docstring: Build cross-file call graph for the project.
decorators: mcp.tool(description='Build full cross-file call graph. Expensive — prefer impact() or context() for targeted queries.')

functions.name: imports
params: file: Annotated[str, Field(description='Path to source file')]
functions.docstring: Parse imports from a source file.
decorators: mcp.tool(description='Parse imports from a source file. Built-in Read is usually sufficient; use this for structured import data.')

functions.name: importers
params: module: Annotated[str, Field(description='Module name to search for')]
functions.docstring: Find all files that import a given module.
decorators: mcp.tool(description='Find all files that import a given module. Use BEFORE renaming or moving a module to know what breaks.')

functions.name: semantic
params: query: Annotated[str, Field(description="Natural language query (e.g. 'authentication logic')")]
k: Annotated[int, Field(description='Number of results to return')] = 10
functions.docstring: Semantic code search using embeddings.
decorators: mcp.tool(description='Search code by meaning using embeddings. ~300 tokens. Finds related functions/classes even without exact keywords. Use INSTEAD OF Grep when searching for a concept rather than literal text.')

functions.name: semantic_index
params: rebuild: Annotated[bool, Field(description='Force full rebuild (ignore incremental)')] = False
functions.docstring: Build or update the semantic search index.
decorators: mcp.tool(description='Build or rebuild the semantic search index. Run once per project (or after major refactors) before using semantic(). Supports FAISS (default) and ColBERT backends.')

functions.name: semantic_info
functions.docstring: Get semantic index info (backend, model, count).
decorators: mcp.tool(description='Get semantic index metadata — backend type, model, unit count, index path. Use to check if an index exists and which backend is active.')

functions.name: diagnostics
params: path: Annotated[str, Field(description='File or directory path')]
functions.docstring: Get type and lint diagnostics.
decorators: mcp.tool(description='Run type checker + linter (pyright + ruff for Python). Validate changes before committing.')

functions.name: change_impact
params: files: Annotated[list[str] | None, Field(description='Changed files (auto-detects from git if None)')] = None
functions.docstring: Find tests affected by changed files.
decorators: mcp.tool(description='Find tests affected by changed files via call graph. Run only relevant tests after edits instead of the full suite.')

functions.name: delegate
params: task: Annotated[str, Field(description="What you're trying to accomplish")]
current_context: Annotated[list[str] | None, Field(description='Symbols already retrieved (avoids re-retrieval)')] = None
budget: Annotated[int, Field(description='Maximum tokens for context')] = 8000
focus: Annotated[list[str] | None, Field(description='Specific files/modules to focus on')] = None
functions.docstring: Get an incremental context retrieval plan instead of raw context.
decorators: mcp.tool(description='Get a prioritized retrieval plan for a complex task instead of fetching all context upfront. Reduces wasted retrieval by 50%+. Returns ordered steps to execute.')

functions.name: verify_coherence
params: files: Annotated[list[str] | None, Field(description='Files to check (auto-detects from git if None)')] = None
functions.docstring: Verify cross-file coherence of recent edits.
decorators: mcp.tool(description="Check cross-file consistency after multi-file edits — signature mismatches, missing imports. Run BEFORE committing when you've changed function signatures or moved code.")

functions.name: diff_context
params: preset: Annotated[str, Field(description='Output preset (compact, minimal, multi-turn)')] = 'compact'
base: Annotated[str | None, Field(description='Git base ref (default: HEAD~1)')] = None
head: Annotated[str | None, Field(description='Git head ref (default: HEAD)')] = None
budget: Annotated[int | None, Field(description='Override token budget from preset')] = None
session_id: Annotated[str | None, Field(description='Session ID for delta caching')] = None
delta: Annotated[bool, Field(description='Track unchanged symbols with [UNCHANGED] markers')] = False
max_lines: Annotated[int | None, Field(description='Cap output at N lines')] = None
max_bytes: Annotated[int | None, Field(description='Cap output at N bytes')] = None
functions.docstring: Get git-aware diff context with symbol mapping and adaptive windowing.
decorators: mcp.tool(description='Git-aware context for recent changes — maps diff hunks to symbols, follows callers. ~800 tokens vs ~4000+ for git diff + Read. Start here for any task involving recently modified code.')

functions.name: structural_search
params: pattern: Annotated[str, Field(description='ast-grep pattern with meta-variables ($VAR, $$$ARGS)')]
language: Annotated[str | None, Field(description='Language (auto-detected if None)')] = None
max_results: Annotated[int, Field(description='Maximum matches to return')] = 50
functions.docstring: Search for structural code patterns using ast-grep (tree-sitter CSTs).
decorators: mcp.tool(description="Search for AST patterns using tree-sitter. Matches code structure, not text. Use INSTEAD OF Grep for queries like 'functions returning None' or 'all method calls'. Meta-variables: $VAR=any node, $$$ARGS=variadic. Example: 'def $FUNC($$$ARGS): return None'")

functions.name: distill
params: task: Annotated[str, Field(description="What you're trying to accomplish")]
budget: Annotated[int, Field(description='Token budget for output')] = 1500
session_id: Annotated[str | None, Field(description='Session ID for delta tracking')] = None
language: Annotated[str | None, Field(description='Programming language')] = None
functions.docstring: Get compressed, prescriptive context for a task.
decorators: mcp.tool(description='Compressed prescriptive context for a task — files to edit, key signatures, risks. Ideal for sub-agent handoff or when you need a plan before diving into code.')

functions.name: hotspots
params: top_n: Annotated[int, Field(description='Number of top symbols to return')] = 20
since_days: Annotated[int | None, Field(description='Only symbols accessed within N days')] = None
functions.docstring: Show frequently accessed symbols across sessions (attention hotspots).
decorators: mcp.tool(description='Show most-accessed symbols across sessions. Requires .tldrs/attention.db. Admin/debugging tool.')

functions.name: status
functions.docstring: Get daemon status including uptime and cache statistics.
decorators: mcp.tool(description='Daemon status — uptime and cache statistics. Admin/debugging tool.')

functions.name: main
functions.docstring: Entry point for tldr-mcp command.
